description: Bayesian model averaging — compute pseudo-BMA weights from an information criterion and form a weighted average of posterior-predictive draws across saved PyMC/ArviZ InferenceData .nc files.
rules: read-only — never mutates inputs or any remote/live service
each input is a netCDF InferenceData file carrying posterior_predictive/predictions for VAR_NAME
MODELS_DIR globs *.nc (model name = filename stem); MODELS overrides with a comma list of name=path or path
VAR_NAME selects the predicted variable (default y_obs); IC selects loo (default) or waic for weights
requires arviz + numpy (+ pymc) importable for real output; absent libs produce a graceful {} stub
weights + averaged-prediction summary written to record_store as averaging.json; the full averaged array is saved alongside as averaging_predictions.npy
usage: Set MODELS_DIR (a dir of .nc files) or MODELS (comma list of name=path.nc). Optional VAR_NAME (default y_obs) + IC (loo|waic). Outputs: averaging.json + averaging_predictions.npy.
limitation: Read-only averaging. Each model needs posterior_predictive (or predictions) for VAR_NAME and compatible draw shapes. Needs arviz/numpy/pymc + the netCDF backend; offline it writes an empty JSON stub.
